meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Struggling with no project ideas? Learn to use Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to validate SaaS concepts and ship your MVP faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is solved by building small, verifiable utilities. Use AI to scan Git repositories for high-friction workflows, then leverage Claude Code to iterate on micro-SaaS solutions. By focusing on rapid deployment rather than original inspiration, you build the muscle memory required to identify profitable market gaps.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You stare at the blinking cursor, waiting for a lightning-bolt epiphany that never strikes, while the market moves on without you. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a systematic feedback loop to transform raw curiosity into a functional, revenue-generating asset.

Git isn't just for version control—it’s a data mine of human pain points. By combining the structural intelligence of Antigravity with the autonomous execution of Claude Code, you can turn stagnant repositories into a live sandbox for rapid prototyping. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the architecture that reveals the opportunity.

steps.title: Identify Market Friction via GitHub
steps.description: Scan popular open-source repositories for 'issue' patterns. High volume of unresolved bugs or feature requests indicates a market gap waiting for a paid solution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 5 trending repositories in the [Niche] category. Identify recurring user pain points from issues and PR discussions that could be solved by a standalone micro-SaaS.

steps.title: Initialize the Lean MVP Structure
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold a production-ready boilerplate. Don't waste time on configuration; focus on the core value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal SaaS architecture using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include authentication and a basic dashboard shell.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold project with nextjs and supabase'

steps.title: Automate Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your core business logic. Focus on the 'One Thing' your product does better than anything else.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust function to handle [Core Feature] and ensure it follows clean architecture principles for future scalability.

steps.title: Rapid Iteration and Git Commit Cycles
steps.description: Establish a rhythm of 'Build-Test-Commit'. Use AI to document your changes automatically, keeping your repository audit-ready for investors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the last 3 files changed. Generate a concise, professional commit message and summarize the impact on the MVP roadmap.

steps.title: Validation Deployment
steps.description: Push your MVP to a live environment. Real usage data is the only metric that matters for a founder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script for Vercel and outline a 3-step plan to gather user feedback from the first 10 beta testers.

pro_tips: Never build a backend for a feature until you have at least 5 people asking for it; use a mock API first.
Use Git tags to mark 'Validation Milestones' so you can pivot without losing your previous architectural progress.
Claude Code performs best when you provide it with a 'Context File' that outlines your project constraints and tech stack preferences.
Analyze your own Git history to identify which types of features you build fastest—double down on that domain.
Don't optimize for code quality in the MVP; optimize for 'time-to-first-revenue'.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, monitor your Git commit frequency. If you stop committing, you've lost product-market fit. Use the feedback from your first 10 users to iterate the product or kill it and start the next idea.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the process, and the technical edge. The only thing missing is the first commit. Stop overthinking the 'perfect' idea and start engineering the solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
